parsed.summary: The cumulative v0.4.0→v0.4.6 diff deletes scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines removed) while simultaneously modifying at least 8 other core runtime modules that could have imported from it. Without verification that no dangling import references remain, this is a startup-crash risk. The diff also introduces 370+ lines of new retry/error-dispatch logic (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) with no evidence of integration testing against the actual glm-client call paths that will exercise them under failure.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs may leave dangling imports across modified core modules
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs was removed entirely (−67 lines) in the cumulative diff. In the same change, scripts/glm-companion.mjs (±139 lines), scripts/lib/glm-client.mjs (±155 lines), scripts/lib/render.mjs (±79 lines), and other core files were modified. If any of these files—or any unchanged file—still contains `import ... from './process.mjs'` or `from './lib/process.mjs'`, the tool will crash at module load time with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND. scripts/check-imports.mjs was modified (±5 lines), suggesting the import checker itself needed adjustment, but there is no CI gate or evidence that a clean import check was run post-deletion against the full tree. This is a binary works/doesn't-work defect: if a dangling import exists, every invocation fails immediately with no fallback.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r "process\.mjs" scripts/` from the repository root. If any hits remain in runtime code (not test mocks), either restore process.mjs or redirect the imports. Add a CI step in pr-check.yml that fails on unresolvable imports to prevent regression.

findings.title: New retry.mjs (203 lines) and bigmodel-errors.mjs (168 lines) lack integration-level validation against real API failure modes
findings.body: retry.mjs adds 203 lines of backoff/retry logic and bigmodel-errors.mjs adds 168 lines of error classification. Both are new modules with unit tests (352 and 189 lines respectively) but the unit tests likely exercise the modules in isolation. The commit message for e0a9ba8 says 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff,' meaning glm-client.mjs (±155 lines changed) now depends on both. If the error classifier mis-handles an error shape returned by the actual API (e.g., a network timeout that surfaces as a different exception type than expected), the retry logic may either (a) retry infinitely on non-retryable errors or (b) give up immediately on retryable ones. The unit tests cannot catch this because they mock the error shapes. No UAT or integration test evidence covers the retry path under real API failure.
findings.recommendation: Add an integration test or UAT scenario that forces a real or simulated API error (e.g., 429 rate-limit, 503, network timeout) and asserts that: (1) retry fires the correct number of times, (2) backoff intervals are respected, and (3) the job eventually fails with a clear user-facing error after exhausting retries. Validate that bigmodel-errors.mjs correctly classifies each error type the API can return.

findings.title: Breadth of change across state management risks silent data corruption on upgrade from v0.4.0
findings.body: state.mjs (±32 lines), job-control.mjs (±20 lines), tracked-jobs.mjs (±78 lines), and fs.mjs (±44 lines) were all modified. These modules manage persisted job state on disk. If the on-disk state format changed between v0.4.0 and v0.4.6 (e.g., new fields, renamed keys, different file structure), users upgrading from v0.4.0 will have stale state files that the new code may misinterpret—silently dropping jobs, corrupting tracking data, or throwing unhandled errors mid-operation. No migration or schema-version guard is visible in the diff stat, and no test file appears to cover upgrade-path resilience.
findings.recommendation: Verify that state.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs handle v0.4.0-format state files gracefully (or at minimum detect and reject them with a clear error message). If the format changed, add a migration path or a version marker to the state file so future changes are backward-compatible.

next_steps: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/` and confirm zero runtime import hits before merge.
Add at least one integration/UAT scenario covering retry behavior under simulated API failure (429, timeout, malformed response).
Verify state file backward compatibility by loading a v0.4.0-era state file against the current code and asserting no crash or silent data loss.
If any of the above checks fail, do not ship until resolved—these are startup-crash or silent-corruption class defects.
